name: Food Technology
description: Food Technology applies food science and engineering principles to processing, preservation, packaging, and quality assurance of food products. It covers the entire food supply chain from raw material handling to consumer-ready products.

subjects.name: Food Chemistry
subjects.description: Study of chemical composition of foods including carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, vitamins, and minerals. Covers chemical reactions during processing, enzymatic and non-enzymatic browning, and food additives.
learning_outcomes: Analyze the chemical composition and functional properties of food components
Predict chemical changes during food processing and storage
Apply knowledge of enzymatic and non-enzymatic browning in product development
Evaluate the role of food additives in product quality and safety
subjects.industry_relevance: Fundamental for product development, quality control, and regulatory compliance in the food industry.

subjects.name: Food Microbiology
subjects.description: Study of microorganisms in food including bacteria, yeasts, molds, and viruses. Covers food spoilage, foodborne pathogens, fermentation, and microbiological testing methods for food safety.
learning_outcomes: Identify foodborne pathogens and their growth requirements
Apply microbiological testing methods for food safety assessment
Control microbial spoilage through preservation techniques
Design fermentation processes for food product development
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for food safety management systems, HACCP implementation, and regulatory compliance worldwide.

subjects.name: Food Processing
subjects.description: Covers unit operations in food processing including size reduction, mixing, separation, evaporation, drying, and extrusion. Focuses on processing parameters affecting product quality, yield, and energy efficiency.
learning_outcomes: Design unit operations for food processing lines
Optimize processing parameters for maximum yield and quality
Calculate mass and energy balances for food processes
Evaluate the impact of processing on nutritional quality
subjects.industry_relevance: Central to all food manufacturing operations from beverages to snacks to dairy products.

subjects.name: Food Engineering
subjects.description: Application of engineering principles to food processing including heat transfer, fluid flow, mass transfer, and thermodynamics. Covers process design, equipment selection, and plant layout for food industries.
learning_outcomes: Apply heat and mass transfer principles to food process equipment design
Design fluid handling systems for viscous food products
Calculate thermal processing requirements for food preservation
Optimize plant layout for food manufacturing facilities
subjects.industry_relevance: Core competency for food process engineers designing equipment and plants for large-scale food production.

subjects.name: Food Preservation
subjects.description: Study of food preservation methods including thermal processing, freezing, dehydration, osmotic dehydration, irradiation, and high-pressure processing. Covers preservation kinetics and shelf life prediction.
learning_outcomes: Design thermal processing schedules for commercial sterilization
Apply emerging non-thermal preservation technologies
Predict shelf life using accelerated storage studies
Optimize freezing and dehydration processes for product quality
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for reducing food waste and extending shelf life across the global food supply chain.

subjects.name: Dairy Technology
subjects.description: Study of milk processing and dairy product manufacturing including pasteurization, homogenization, cheese making, yogurt, butter, and milk powder production. Covers quality standards and dairy plant sanitation.
learning_outcomes: Design milk processing lines for various dairy products
Control fermentation parameters for cheese and yogurt production
Apply hygiene and sanitation protocols in dairy plants
Evaluate dairy product quality using physicochemical analysis
subjects.industry_relevance: Dairy is one of the largest food sectors globally with continuous demand for processed dairy products.

subjects.name: Meat Technology
subjects.description: Covers meat science, slaughterhouse operations, meat processing, and product manufacturing including sausages, ham, and meat emulsions. Includes meat inspection, preservation, and by-product utilization.
learning_outcomes: Evaluate meat quality based on carcass grading and composition
Design processed meat formulations for sausages and restructured products
Apply preservation techniques including curing, smoking, and chilling
Implement HACCP plans in meat processing facilities
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for the multi-billion dollar meat processing industry including fresh and value-added products.

subjects.name: Bakery Technology
subjects.description: Study of bakery product manufacturing including bread, cakes, biscuits, pastries, and cookies. Covers ingredient functionality, dough rheology, baking processes, and quality evaluation of bakery products.
learning_outcomes: Analyze ingredient functionality in bakery product formulations
Control fermentation and proofing conditions for consistent quality
Design bakery production lines for industrial scale operations
Troubleshoot common bakery product defects
subjects.industry_relevance: The global bakery market drives demand for technologists in industrial bakeries and ingredient supply companies.

subjects.name: Fruit & Vegetable Technology
subjects.description: Covers post-harvest handling, storage, and processing of fruits and vegetables including canning, juice production, drying, and freezing. Includes minimal processing and value-added product development.
learning_outcomes: Design post-harvest handling systems to minimize losses
Develop fruit and vegetable products including juices and concentrates
Apply controlled atmosphere storage for extended shelf life
Optimize blanching and drying conditions for nutritional retention
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for reducing post-harvest losses and meeting demand for processed fruit and vegetable products.

subjects.name: Food Packaging
subjects.description: Study of food packaging materials, package design, and packaging technologies including aseptic packaging, modified atmosphere packaging, and active packaging. Covers shelf life extension and sustainability considerations.
learning_outcomes: Select appropriate packaging materials for different food products
Design modified atmosphere packaging for extended shelf life
Evaluate barrier properties of packaging films
Assess environmental impact of packaging and explore sustainable alternatives
subjects.industry_relevance: Packaging innovation drives food safety, shelf life extension, and sustainability in the global food industry.

subjects.name: Food Quality Control
subjects.description: Covers quality assurance systems including HACCP, ISO 22000, GMP, and GHP for food safety management. Includes statistical process control, sensory evaluation, food adulteration testing, and regulatory compliance.
learning_outcomes: Develop and implement HACCP plans for food processing facilities
Apply statistical process control for quality monitoring
Detect food adulteration using analytical techniques
Audit food safety management systems for regulatory compliance
subjects.industry_relevance: Mandatory for all food businesses to ensure product safety and meet regulatory standards globally.

subjects.name: Food Additives
subjects.description: Study of food additives including preservatives, antioxidants, emulsifiers, stabilizers, colors, flavors, and sweeteners. Covers regulatory approval processes, safety evaluation, and applications in food product development.
learning_outcomes: Classify food additives based on function and regulatory status
Evaluate safety through ADI and toxicological data interpretation
Select appropriate additives for specific food applications
Comply with labeling regulations for additive declaration
subjects.industry_relevance: The food additives industry supplies essential ingredients to all food manufacturing sectors worldwide.

subjects.name: Nutrition Science
subjects.description: Study of human nutrition including macronutrients, micronutrients, digestion, absorption, and metabolism. Covers nutritional requirements across life stages, dietary guidelines, and functional foods for health promotion.
learning_outcomes: Calculate nutritional requirements for different population groups
Evaluate the nutritional impact of food processing methods
Design functional foods for specific health benefits
Analyze food labels for nutritional content and health claims
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing consumer focus on health drives demand for nutrition expertise in product development and wellness sectors.

subjects.name: Food Biotechnology
subjects.description: Covers applications of biotechnology in food processing including genetic engineering, enzyme technology, fermentation, and starter culture development. Includes GM foods, biofortification, and regulatory aspects.
learning_outcomes: Apply enzyme technology for food processing improvements
Design fermentation processes using genetically modified microorganisms
Evaluate safety and regulatory aspects of genetically modified foods
Develop starter cultures for fermented food products
subjects.industry_relevance: Driving innovation in enzyme production, fermentation, and development of novel food ingredients.

subjects.name: Food Plant Design
subjects.description: Study of food plant layout, equipment selection, utilities, and sanitary design principles. Covers plant location, process flow diagrams, material handling, waste management, and regulatory compliance for food facilities.
learning_outcomes: Design sanitary food plant layouts meeting regulatory requirements
Select equipment based on process requirements and hygienic design
Design utility systems including steam, water, and refrigeration
Develop waste treatment and by-product utilization strategies
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for food industry expansion and new facility construction meeting modern safety standards.

subjects.name: Sensory Evaluation
subjects.description: Covers sensory analysis methods including discrimination tests, descriptive analysis, and consumer testing. Includes panel selection, test room design, statistical analysis, and sensory-instrumental correlations.
learning_outcomes: Design and conduct discrimination and descriptive sensory tests
Train and manage sensory panel performance
Apply statistical methods for sensory data analysis
Correlate sensory attributes with instrumental measurements
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for product development, quality maintenance, and consumer acceptance testing in food companies.

subjects.name: Cereal Technology
subjects.description: Study of cereal grains including wheat, rice, corn, barley, and oats and their processing into flour, breakfast cereals, pasta, and snacks. Covers milling technology, grain storage, and quality assessment.
learning_outcomes: Design milling processes for wheat and rice
Develop breakfast cereal and snack food formulations
Optimize pasta and noodle manufacturing processes
Evaluate grain quality parameters for processing suitability
subjects.industry_relevance: Cereals are the most important food staple globally, supporting massive milling and processing industries.

subjects.name: Beverage Technology
subjects.description: Study of beverage manufacturing including carbonated soft drinks, fruit juices, tea, coffee, beer, wine, and bottled water. Covers formulation, carbonation, fermentation, filtration, and packaging of beverages.
learning_outcomes: Formulate carbonated and non-carbonated beverages
Design beer and wine fermentation processes
Apply filtration and stabilization techniques for beverage clarity
Evaluate beverage quality through physicochemical analysis
subjects.industry_relevance: The global beverage industry encompasses soft drinks, alcoholic beverages, and bottled water worth trillions of dollars.
